• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples
  • Data Structures
  • Data Fields

Data Structures

Here are the data structures with brief descriptions:
c2c_pkt_headerWe need to embed a chip-to-chip packet header in the beginning of each packet for the following reasons: -- to transport the size of packet data payload to the receiver, in the absence of dedicated metadata packets
dsa_signature_tThe dsa_signature record type is used to package up the two large integer values (called 'r' and 's' in the DSA standard) into a DSA signature
DynamicHeaderFinal routing destinations for dynamic messages
DynamicHeader::_bitsUseful for setting route parameters
ecc_curve_tThe ecc_curve_t record type is used to hold all of the parameters defining an elliptic curve over a large prime number finite field
ecc_point_tThe ecc_point_t record type is used to represent a point on an elliptic curve defined over a finite field
gxcr_aead_context_tData structure that encapsulates a pointer to the memory that is to be used with the shim for the metadata, and the information about where the token and the context record fields are within that memory
gxcr_aead_params_tParameters used to set up an AEAD context
gxcr_contextA context object that encapsulates data to be sent to the crypto acceleration hardware, including metadata related to the algorithm, and input and result data for the algorithm
gxcr_ipsec_params_tParameters used to set up an IPsec SA
gxcr_ipsec_sa_tData structure that encapsulates a pointer to the memory that is to be used with shim for the metadata, and the information about where the token and the context record fields are within that memory
gxcr_result_token_tThe result from the Packet Processing engine
gxio_gpio_context_tA context object used to manage GPIO hardware resources
gxio_mica_context_tA context object used to manage MiCA hardware resources
gxio_mica_pka_context_tA context object used to manage MiCA PKA hardware resources
gxio_mica_pka_ring_config_tDescribes how to configure a PKA engine's command result rings
gxio_mica_pka_trng_context_tA context object used to manage MiCA PKA TRNG hardware resources
gxio_mica_result_tGxio_mica_result_t
gxio_mpipe_context_tA context object used to manage mPIPE hardware resources
gxio_mpipe_equeue_tA convenient, thread-safe interface to an eDMA ring
gxio_mpipe_iqueue_tA convenient interface to a NotifRing, for use by a single thread
gxio_mpipe_link_tAn object used to manage mPIPE link state and resources
gxio_mpipe_notif_group_bits_tBit vector encoding which NotifRings are in a NotifGroup
gxio_mpipe_rules_dmac_tA destination mac address
gxio_mpipe_rules_stacks_tEight buffer stack ids
gxio_mpipe_rules_tA set of classifier rules, plus a context
gxio_mpipe_stats_tMPIPE statistics structure
gxio_trio_context_tA context object used to manage TRIO hardware resources
gxio_trio_dma_queue_tA convenient, thread-safe interface to an eDMA ring
gxio_usb_dev_context_tA context object used to manage USB hardware resources
gxpci_cmd_tA structure used to post zero-copy commands
gxpci_comp_tThe structure returned when a command completes
gxpci_context_sA gxpci_context_t describes a unidirectional ZC queue
gxpci_dma_cmd_tA structure used to post DMA commands
gxpci_nic_comp_tThe structure returned when a host NIC command completes
gxpci_nic_state_sA gxpci_nic_state_t describes the NIC-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t
gxpci_packet_queue_stateThis describes the Packet Queue unidirectional queue state
gxpci_raw_dma_state_sThis describes the Raw DMA channel-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t
gxpci_resource_sA gxpci_resource_t describes the resources that are allocated to a ZC client
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound_sa_tData structure that encapsulates general IPsec SA and operation-specific parameters
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_tData structure that encapsulates general IPsec SA and operation-specific parameters
MICA_COMP_CTX_USER_OPCODE_tOpcode
MICA_CRYPTO_CTX_USER_OPCODE_tOpcode
mica_ctxt_req_tMica_ctxt_req_t
MICA_OPCODE_tOpcode
mica_queue_stats_tRecord for receiving mica_queue stats
MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_tMPIPE eDMA Descriptor
MPIPE_PDESC_tMPIPE Packet Descriptor
pka_cmd_desc_tA PKA command descriptor
pka_operand_tThe pka_operand_t is the record type used to represent big integer numbers
pka_operands_tThe pka_operands_t record type is used to package the entire set of input operands (big integers) of a single crypto operation
pka_res_desc_tThe result descriptor
pka_results_tThe pka_results_t record type is used to package up the entire set of output operands (of which there can be 0, 1 or 2) as well as other result values like the compare_result (only used for compare op), the result_cnt and the overall status
pka_vector_tA data vector
tmc_alloc_tDescribe an allocation
tmc_interrupt_funcTmc_interrupt_func_t is the user-space C callback function which can have two different types at the same time
tmc_perf_countersThe counters are 32-bits, so for example, the TMC_PERF_ONE event counter will overflow in 3.5 seconds at 1.2 GHz
tmc_spin_barrier_tA shared memory, spin-based barrier structure
tmc_spin_cond_tA shared memory, spin-based condition variable
tmc_spin_mutex_tA shared memory spinlock data structure
tmc_spin_queued_mutex_tA shared memory, spinning, fair mutex
tmc_spin_rwlock_tA shared memory reader/writer spinlock data structure
tmc_sync_barrier_tA shared memory barrier data structure
TRIO_DMA_DESC_tTRIO DMA Descriptor

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
